notes: Official homepage; navigation source for admissions, academics, resources, and current site structure.
States HU uses the U.S. higher-education model, English as language of instruction, and offers MA and MBA degrees.
Primary graduate program index; lists MBA and MA offerings with credits and degree labels.
Official program PDF for MBA General Business Administration; includes MBA overview, admissions background, credits, prerequisites, core courses, thesis.
Official program PDF for MBA Accounting; also includes shared MBA overview and Finance section.
Official program PDF for MBA Finance; same catalog extract as Accounting/Finance page.
Official program PDF for MBA Human Resources Management; also includes Management section.
Official program PDF for MBA Management; duplicated extract with HRM/Management content.
Official program PDF for MBA Marketing.
Official program PDF for MA Education.
Official program PDF for MA Psychology.
Graduate rules: time limits, normal progress, grading, graduation, MA and MBA academic rules.
Graduate handbook landing page; links to handbook PDF and thesis appendices.
Official student graduate program handbook with thesis process and graduate study regulations.
Graduate admissions landing page; sparse content and deadline widget.
Graduate admissions categories: regular graduate students, undergraduate students, special students, auditors, transfer students, re-admission.
General admissions philosophy and categories; official HU admissions source.
Online application landing page and general HU positioning.
Tuition/fee policy: payment deadlines, installment payments, annual review, 2025-2026 10% subsidy.
Graduate tuition/fees page; visible extracted text mainly delinquent payment policy.
Graduate tuition calculator landing page; no machine-readable rates found in extracted content.
General financial aid policy; annual aid for regular full-time students, good-standing requirement, work program.
Financial aid application document list and application notes.
Need-based aid and academic scholarship types; includes percentage ranges and scholarship notes.
FAQ includes explicit graduate aid eligibility: full-time graduate students may apply, limited recipients, priority by demonstrated need.
Other sources of additional aid; mostly external funds, so not used for official HU program seeding except as advisory context.
Financial Aid Office contact information.
Academic calendar index; includes 2026-2027 and 2025-2026 calendar PDFs/links.
Application, transcript, financial aid, other, graduate, and financial certificate forms; graduate thesis appendices.
International-student page; appears undergraduate/special-category focused, no graduate-specific international policy found.
Faculty listing; identifies Faculty of Business Administration and Economics and School of Arts and Sciences subdivisions including Social and Behavioral Sciences.
Official catalog PDF; includes graduate catalog section, admissions, financial information, academic information, MBA and MA programs, language/institution profile.
